title: Mom's iCloud takeover note — 'Yahir take ur notes off my iPad'
html:
TL;DR

  • A one-paragraph plea from Iracema to Yahir, dictated/typed directly into Yahir's own Notes app via the shared iCloud account, asking him to come unlink his iCloud from her iPad — the irony being that the only way she could reach him was by writing in his notes.
  • This is a load-bearing artifact for the thesis: Yahir's notes were so syncretic across the family that mom used them as a chat channel.
  • Tone: 5-emoji-SOS opener (🆘🆘🆘🆘🆘), affectionate close ("Love ya"). Pure mom-text energy translated to the wrong app.
  • Captures the mid-2020 COVID family-tech entanglement — everyone in the same house, sharing devices, with Yahir as the household's de-facto IT admin.
  • Pairs naturally with the Charmin stamping route note from earlier the same year — both show Iracema using Yahir's phone-notes as her own scratchpad.

Key quote

"🆘🆘🆘🆘🆘 Yahir I told you to come take off ur off ur iCloud from the iPad bc I don't wanna see ur notes every time I go to make a note and then I don't end up making a note. so pls come by ASAP to take it off. Thanks. Love ya."

Why it matters in the graph

  • Direct evidence that the is literally a shared family substrate, not a private journal — meaning the privacy norms in this vault have to assume cross-family access at the source. That changes how aggressively to redact.
  • One of the rare appearances of Iracema's own voice (rather than Yahir ghostwriting in her voice as in 2021 05 30 iracema graduation letter). Texturally distinct: emojis, lowercase, "ur".
  • Mid-COVID date (July 17, 2020) — places everyone home together, shared iPad, multi-device iCloud spillover. The friction is intimate and pandemic-coded.
  • Comedic: it's a request to stop using his iCloud, written via his iCloud. Self-undermining as artifact.

Connections / wikilinks
